dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8987 IH: Foreign Service Workforce Retention Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Olszewski (for himself and Mr. Bera) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Foreign Service Act of 1980 relating to the recall and reappointment of career members.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Foreign Service Workforce Retention Act.

section.header: Amendments to the Foreign Service Act of 1980
section.text: Section 308 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 (22 U.S.C. 3948) is amended—

clause.text: by inserting or separated after any retired; and
by inserting or separation after of retirement; and
by inserting or separated after A retired;
by inserting or separated after a retired; and
by inserting or separation after of retirement;

subparagraph.text: by striking Former career members and inserting the following:
in paragraph (1), as so designated, by inserting , for a period of five years after the date of retirement or separation, after without regard to section 306; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: A member seeking reappointment in the Service under paragraph (1) shall be reappointed not later than 180 days after the date of the approval of such request for reappointment to the Department and shall be incorporated into the next onward assignment application cycle of the Service with full standing.
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of the enactment of this subsection, and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate a report detailing—

paragraph.text: the number of career members of the Service who were recalled and reappointed under this section during the preceding fiscal year;
the grade of each such member at the time of their retirement or separation; and
the grade and position of each such member at the time of their recall or reappointment.
